mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the accommodation zone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-11-01 indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points signals strengthening labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% on 2020-11-01 reflects post-shock recovery phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 2% signals strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 553,329M on 2020-10-01 reflects pandemic-driven demand volatility
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month moving average while remaining below pre-shock levels, signaling supply-side recovery momentum that supports equity valuations through improved capacity utilization and profit margins over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.98 on 2020-11-01 shows recovery from pandemic trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in recovery periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, signaling broad-based demand strength across consumer and residential sectors that drives positive equity returns. This interaction captures the joint signal of synchronized demand expansion over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 1% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,530K and RSAFS at 553,329M on 2020-10-01 show synchronized recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 62% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K per month while the unemployment rate remains elevated above 5%, signaling labor market tightening that increases wage pressure and corporate earnings uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in late-cycle conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,629K and UNRATE at 6.7% on 2020-11-01
regime_hint: late-cycle labor tightening with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, signaling monetary policy lag that creates real rate compression. This regime produces negative equity returns as markets anticipate future rate hikes over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.82 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-11-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 5% month-over-month, signaling demand weakness that precedes equity market downturns. This regime-specific mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in late-cycle slowdown conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,583.5B and HOUST at 1,530K on 2020-10-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in slowdown periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
